summary:
Neither type involves a reaction between acrylic acids and dental minerals; instead, their bond formation relies mainly on physical interactions without such chemistry-specific bonds typical of some others like glass-ionomers. Agent1 identified that neither material uses an acrylate-based mechanism involving polymer chains linking through reactions at mineral surfaces characteristic only certain restorative materials but shared more broadly among many indirect retainers including ZOE or PCCs here discussed. Hence selecting 'no' aligning well since none exhibit this particular feature distinguishing them clearly against alternatives presented while fitting contextually within broader understanding The summary should be concise:

Neither type forms chemical bonds using acrylic acids with dental minerals; both use mechanical attachment for strength. \boxed{B}

This version captures the critical point succinctly related to the absence of similar chemical bonding processes in either cement listed.
